summary_oneline: Cross-session Postgres+S3 catalog for security reports, populated by scanner toolkits via a mixin and consumed via a new SecurityReportToolkit, with fractal weekly/monthly summaries — brainstorm architecture sound, codebase contract needs ~10 corrections before specification.

localization.role: Live integration target. Hosts the existing scanner toolkit wiring, BACKSTORY block, and @schedule'd tasks. NOTE: file is gitignored AND contains a broken consolidate_weekly_security_summary stub at lines 445-471 referencing yet-to-exist symbols (self._report_store, ReportFilter, self._build_weekly_summary).

Canonical FileManager surface (April 2026 migration from parrot/tools/file/*). Method names are upload_file, download_file, exists, get_file_url — NOT upload/download/get_url as the brainstorm claimed.
Factory entry point. Accepts manager_type plus per-impl kwargs. S3FileManager takes aws_id (key into navconfig AWS_CREDENTIALS) or a credentials dict; there is NO `aws_config` kwarg.

Real location of AbstractToolkit (NOT parrot/tools/abstract). Auto-discovers async public methods; lifecycle exclusion list at lines 368-403: get_tools, get_tools_filtered, get_tools_sync, get_tool, list_tool_names, start, stop, cleanup, plus an exclude_tools tuple consumed by the discovery walk.
Module that exports the tool-result types only (NOT the toolkit base — see preceding row).
ScheduleType enum (has WEEKLY and MONTHLY members); @schedule decorator passes arbitrary kwargs through to the scheduler, so day_of_week, day, hour, minute are accepted verbatim.
Decorator factory for AbstractBot subclasses, accepts at_startup=True via **kwargs. SecurityAgent is already registered.

Scanner toolkit. PUBLIC scan methods are run_scan(plugins, ignore_ok, suppress, config) and run_compliance_scan(framework, ignore_ok=True, config=None) — both return Pydantic ScanResult, NOT dict. Output-format conversion lives in a separate generate_report(format=...) method. FEAT-160 (merged today) added CloudSploitConfig.config_file plus per-call config arg.

Compliance toolkit (orchestrates Prowler+Trivy+Checkov, returns ConsolidatedReport). Container toolkit (Trivy filesystem/image; methods prefixed trivy_* — real name is trivy_scan_filesystem, NOT scan_filesystem). Container toolkit captures Trivy via stdout — there is NO results_dir / report_output_dir, so a Path-based content branch in the mixin will not work for Trivy unless we capture stdout to bytes or write a temp file.

localization.symbol: S3_ARTIFACT_BUCKET, AWS_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_SECRET_KEY, AWS_REGION_NAME, default_dsn, AWS_CREDENTIALS
localization.role: Real navconfig surface. Names are AWS_ACCESS_KEY / AWS_SECRET_KEY / default_dsn (NOT AWS_KEY / AWS_SECRET / DEFAULT_PG_DSN as brainstorm assumed). aws_security is an INI SECTION read via config.get('aws_security', '<key>'), not a parrot.conf module constant.
Real attribute name on the bot base class. Set to a GoogleGenAIClient (or other) after super().__init__() in Agent (agents/agent.py:127-131). The brainstorm's `llm_client=self.llm` is correct.
Existing precedent for ThinkingConfig(include_thoughts=False) with structured output. Summarizer module can follow this pattern verbatim.
Peer abstraction. Conversation-scoped artifacts backed by DynamoDB/SQLite + OverflowStore. NOT a replacement target for the new catalog — different lifecycle, different consumers.
Representative example of the project's Postgres-async convention. `from asyncdb import AsyncDB` with `driver='pg', dsn=...`. 30 usages across parrot/ vs 1 raw asyncpg call.

Closest precedent for schema layout: a bare .sql file co-located with the module, using CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + CREATE INDEX. No Python loader reads it; ops applies it out-of-band. No migration framework (no Alembic, no migrations dir) exists.
Pydantic pinned to 2.12.5 — Pydantic v2 is confirmed as the project standard.

constraints.statement: FileManager method names are upload_file / download_file / get_file_url / exists — NOT upload / download / get_url
constraints.implication: ReportPersistenceMixin and PostgresS3SecurityReportStore must call upload_file/download_file/get_file_url. Every code snippet in the brainstorm using `await fm.upload(...)` etc. is wrong and must be re-written in the spec.

constraints.statement: S3FileManager constructor takes aws_id OR a credentials dict — not aws_config
constraints.implication: Spec must replace the brainstorm's `aws_config={...}` kwarg with either (a) `aws_id='security'` keyed into AWS_CREDENTIALS, or (b) `credentials={'aws_key': ..., 'aws_secret': ..., 'region_name': ...}` literal.

constraints.statement: Scanner public methods return Pydantic models, not dict
constraints.implication: ReportPersistenceMixin must serialize via `result.model_dump_json().encode()` for ScanResult and ConsolidatedReport. Brainstorm's `Path` content branch is correct only for CloudSploit (which writes a JSON file before returning the ScanResult).

constraints.statement: CloudSploit has no output_format kwarg on scan methods; the format is selected post-hoc via generate_report(format=...)
constraints.implication: Spec must drop the brainstorm's `output_format='json'` kwarg on run_cloudsploit_scan. Persistence happens against the JSON output regardless; HTML/PDF is a separate side branch.

constraints.statement: ContainerSecurityToolkit has no results_dir — Trivy output is captured from stdout
constraints.implication: Mixin needs a bytes content branch for Trivy (either ContainerSecurityToolkit captures stdout into bytes, or it writes JSON to a temp Path the mixin reads). Spec must pick one.

Spec changes to SecurityAgent are local-only — no CI / no diff review. Worker-side implementation must edit it AND coordinate with ops. Consider committing a redacted template under examples/ or policies/ for traceability.

constraints.statement: agents/security.py already contains a broken consolidate_weekly_security_summary stub at lines 445-471 referencing symbols that do not exist yet
constraints.implication: Spec's FIRST implementation task should be to make the import succeed (either remove the stub, or land the Module 1+2 dependencies before flipping it on). Otherwise the SecurityAgent fails to load mid-feature.

constraints.statement: BACKSTORY already references find_security_report (lines 56-63) even though the tool doesn't exist anywhere
constraints.implication: Live prompt is referencing vapor. Spec must align Module 4 implementation with the existing BACKSTORY shape OR explicitly rewrite the BACKSTORY block in the same atomic change.

constraints.statement: Producer toolkit constructors pass **kwargs through to AbstractToolkit
constraints.implication: ReportPersistenceMixin must pop its own kwargs (file_manager, report_store) BEFORE `super().__init__(**kwargs)` to avoid breaking the parent contract.

constraints.statement: Postgres convention is asyncdb (`from asyncdb import AsyncDB`, driver='pg'), not asyncpg
constraints.implication: PostgresS3SecurityReportStore must use asyncdb. Resolves brainstorm OQ #1.

constraints.statement: No migration framework exists in the project
constraints.implication: Spec follows the bare-.sql convention: ship schema.sql with CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, applied out-of-band by ops. A bootstrap_schema() method on the store is a reasonable, additive new convention. Resolves brainstorm OQ #2.

constraints.statement: AWS credentials for SecurityAgent come from a non-default INI section (`aws_security`), not from AWS_ACCESS_KEY / AWS_SECRET_KEY
constraints.implication: Cleanest spec move: add an `AWS_CREDENTIALS['security']` slot in parrot/conf.py so `FileManagerFactory.create(manager_type='s3', aws_id='security')` is idiomatic. Alternative: read the INI section keys into a credentials dict per call.

constraints.statement: FEAT-160 (cloudsploit-config-support) merged on 2026-05-12, adding CloudSploitConfig.config_file and per-call `config` arg on run_scan/run_compliance_scan
constraints.implication: Spec must align CloudSploit wiring with the new config field. Brainstorm pre-dates this merge.

constraints.statement: ThinkingConfig(include_thoughts=False) is an established pattern in parrot/clients/google/client.py:1957-1977
constraints.implication: Summarizer module follows the same pattern — no new architecture needed for structured output.

constraints.statement: Pydantic v2 (2.12.5) is the project standard
constraints.implication: All new models must use v2 (model_dump, model_dump_json, model_validate). Consistent with brainstorm.

constraints.statement: FEAT-103 ArtifactStore is a peer abstraction with a different lifecycle (DynamoDB/SQLite + OverflowStore, conversation-scoped)
constraints.implication: Spec must explicitly state the new catalog is NOT extending ArtifactStore — confirms brainstorm §2 Non-Goals.

new_components.name: parrot/storage/security_reports/models.py
new_components.role: ReportKind, ReportRef, ReportFilter, SeverityBreakdown, EmbeddedFinding Pydantic v2 models

new_components.name: parrot/storage/security_reports/store.py
new_components.role: SecurityReportStore Protocol + PostgresS3SecurityReportStore impl using asyncdb

new_components.name: parrot/storage/security_reports/schema.sql
new_components.role: Bare DDL following the security_events.sql precedent

new_components.name: parrot_tools/security/parsers/
new_components.role: Per-scanner deterministic Python parsers (Trivy / CloudSploit / Prowler / Checkov / Aggregator)

new_components.name: parrot_tools/security/summarizer.py
new_components.role: WeeklySecuritySummarizer + MonthlySecuritySummarizer (deterministic diffs; LLM only for executive paragraph)

modified_components.change: Add ReportPersistenceMixin to base class chain; thread file_manager/report_store kwargs through __init__ via pop(); auto-persist after run_compliance_scan/run_scan (returning the ScanResult unchanged)
Same mixin pattern. The ConsolidatedReport return shape is preserved; persistence is a side effect
Same mixin pattern. Resolve the no-results_dir issue per the chosen U3 strategy (stdout-to-bytes or temp-file)

Wire FileManagerFactory + PostgresS3SecurityReportStore at __init__; inject into producer toolkits in agent_tools(); add weekly/monthly @schedule'd consolidators; FIX the broken stub at lines 445-471; align BACKSTORY with real Module 4 tool names
Add AWS_CREDENTIALS['security'] slot derived from the aws_security INI section (clean path) — pending U2

untouched: parrot/storage/artifacts.py — FEAT-103 ArtifactStore stays as a peer abstraction (different consumers, conversation scope)
parrot/interfaces/file/* — FileManager surface is consumed as-is; the spec must not reshape it
Existing /tmp/security-reports/ host-filesystem behavior — v1 starts fresh; no migration of legacy reports (brainstorm §11 AC #5)
Other scheduled tasks in SecurityAgent — they auto-populate the catalog as a side effect of the mixin; their signatures and return dicts are preserved (brainstorm §5 Module 7.E)
patterns_to_follow: Bare .sql DDL co-located with the module — match parrot/security/security_events.sql [F016]
ThinkingConfig(include_thoughts=False) for structured Gemini calls — match parrot/clients/google/client.py:1957-1977 [F022]
AbstractToolkit auto-discovery: every async public method on the toolkit (minus the lifecycle exclusion list) becomes a tool exposed to the LLM [F007]
Mixin .pop() pattern for kwargs to keep super().__init__() contract intact [F012]
integration_risks: RISK: agents/security.py is gitignored — implementation cannot rely on diff review and ops must apply the file independently. MITIGATION: U1 resolution (template file under examples/ or policies/).

RISK: Existing broken stub at agents/security.py:445-471 will block import. MITIGATION: spec orders the storage models + store as Tasks 1-2 and lands the consolidator wiring only AFTER they are in place; or the stub is deleted as Task 0.

RISK: Trivy stdout pattern doesn't fit the brainstorm's `content: bytes | Path` mixin signature naturally. MITIGATION: U3 resolution. Strong default: capture stdout into bytes inside ContainerSecurityToolkit, drop the Path branch for Trivy. [F012, F023]

RISK: FEAT-160 just landed; CloudSploitConfig has new fields the brainstorm doesn't reference. MITIGATION: spec explicitly threads CloudSploitConfig.config_file when the SecurityAgent constructs the toolkit. [F019]

RISK: BACKSTORY already references find_security_report — if Module 4 is shipped late, the live prompt instructs the LLM to call a nonexistent tool. MITIGATION: spec must land Module 4 BEFORE keeping the BACKSTORY policy block, or rewrite the block during the SecurityAgent wiring task. [F001]

RISK: Two SeverityLevel-like enums (existing parrot_tools.security.models.SeverityLevel + new SeverityBreakdown) could collide on import. MITIGATION: spec names the new model SeverityBreakdown (count container, not level enum) — distinct enough; document the difference in §6 Codebase Contract. [F013]

confidence_map.statement: The catalog should be implemented at parrot/storage/security_reports/, peer to parrot/storage/artifacts.py
confidence_map.reasoning: Storage layout already houses peer stores; ArtifactStore was added in FEAT-103 with the same parent dir convention.

confidence_map.statement: FileManager methods to call are upload_file / download_file / get_file_url / exists
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read of FileManagerInterface contract.

confidence_map.statement: FileManagerFactory.create() does NOT accept aws_config; brainstorm's S3FileManager kwarg shape is wrong
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read of factory + S3FileManager constructors.

confidence_map.statement: Scanner public methods return Pydantic ScanResult / ConsolidatedReport, not dict
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct reads of toolkit method signatures.

confidence_map.statement: CloudSploit method names are run_scan / run_compliance_scan; output_format is not a scan-method kwarg
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read; brainstorm's run_cloudsploit_scan is fabricated.

confidence_map.statement: ContainerSecurityToolkit captures Trivy via stdout; no results_dir attribute
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read; brainstorm's results_dir assumption fails for Trivy.

confidence_map.statement: agents/security.py is gitignored and contains a broken stub referencing planned symbols
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct file read + git log inspection.

confidence_map.statement: Postgres driver convention is asyncdb (30 usages vs 1 asyncpg)
confidence_map.reasoning: Codebase-wide grep count; resolves brainstorm OQ #1.

confidence_map.reasoning: Codebase search returned no Alembic/migrations dir; security_events.sql is the closest analog. Resolves brainstorm OQ #2.

confidence_map.statement: ScheduleType has WEEKLY and MONTHLY; @schedule passes arbitrary kwargs through, so day_of_week/day/hour/minute are accepted verbatim
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read of decorator.

confidence_map.statement: AbstractToolkit auto-discovery rules confirm Module 4 tools will be exposed by inheriting AbstractToolkit alone (no manual registration needed)
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read of lifecycle exclusion list.

confidence_map.statement: AbstractBot.llm is the live attribute; brainstorm's `llm_client=self.llm` is correct

confidence_map.statement: Adding AWS_CREDENTIALS['security'] to parrot/conf.py would let FileManagerFactory.create(aws_id='security') work idiomatically
confidence_map.reasoning: Pattern-matched against existing AWS_CREDENTIALS slots; not observed elsewhere but consistent with current shape. Could equally be solved by passing a credentials dict per call (U2).

confidence_map.statement: Producer toolkit mixin must pop its kwargs before super().__init__ to preserve **kwargs forwarding to AbstractToolkit
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read of current toolkit constructors.

confidence_map.statement: FEAT-160 (merged today) adds CloudSploitConfig.config_file and per-call config arg — spec must thread the new field

confidence_map.reasoning: ContainerSecurityToolkit has no results_dir/report_output_dir; pipes are stdout-only.

confidence_map.statement: The brainstorm's architectural decisions (Postgres+S3, mixin, fractal summaries, deterministic parsers) remain sound after research
confidence_map.reasoning: Every architectural pillar maps to an existing convention or a clean extension point. Corrections are tactical (paths, method names) — not architectural.

confidence_map.statement: BACKSTORY already mentions find_security_report; without Module 4, the live prompt instructs the LLM to call a nonexistent tool
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read of BACKSTORY block.

unknowns.question: How should we handle traceability for the gitignored agents/security.py — commit a redacted template (no secrets) under examples/agents/ or policies/agents/, or accept that the live file stays untracked and only ops applies it?
plausible_answers: Commit a redacted template under examples/agents/security.py so the spec and code-review process can reason about the wiring.
Keep agents/security.py untracked; the spec describes the wiring in §3 and the implementer + ops coordinate via the spec.
Move SecurityAgent to a tracked location entirely (refactor out of agents/ into parrot/agents/security/) — out of scope for this FEAT.
unknowns.user_answer: Keep agents/security.py untracked. Spec describes wiring in §3 prose; the implementer and ops coordinate via the spec. No new tracked template file in this FEAT.

unknowns.question: Should we add AWS_CREDENTIALS['security'] in parrot/conf.py (so S3FileManager(aws_id='security') is idiomatic), or read the aws_security INI section into a credentials dict at the SecurityAgent construction site?
plausible_answers: Add AWS_CREDENTIALS['security'] slot in parrot/conf.py — most consistent with existing FileManagerFactory ergonomics. [Recommended]
Read aws_security INI keys at SecurityAgent __init__ and pass `credentials={'aws_key': ..., 'aws_secret': ..., 'region_name': ...}` to FileManagerFactory.create.
Reuse the default AWS_CREDENTIALS slot (no aws_security separation) and rely on bucket-level IAM — only if the security bucket really shares creds with everything else.
unknowns.user_answer: Add AWS_CREDENTIALS['security'] slot in parrot/conf.py derived from the aws_security INI section. SecurityAgent uses FileManagerFactory.create(manager_type='s3', aws_id='security', ...).

unknowns.question: For Trivy (no results_dir), should ReportPersistenceMixin always receive content as bytes (captured from stdout inside ContainerSecurityToolkit), or should we add a temp-file write in the toolkit and keep the mixin's `bytes | Path` signature?
plausible_answers: Capture Trivy stdout into bytes inside ContainerSecurityToolkit; mixin keeps `bytes | Path` signature but Trivy always uses the bytes branch. [Recommended — minimal coupling]
Have ContainerSecurityToolkit write a temp file and pass a Path to the mixin — homogeneous with CloudSploit but adds tmp-dir lifecycle for a synthetic file.
Add a third `stream: AsyncIterator[bytes]` content type to the mixin for streaming-friendly scanners — overkill for current scanners.
unknowns.user_answer: ContainerSecurityToolkit writes Trivy stdout to a temp file inside the toolkit. Mixin's content signature stays `bytes | Path`; Trivy always uses the Path branch. ContainerSecurityToolkit owns the tmp-file lifecycle (write, hand to mixin, delete after persist).

unknowns.question: For search_findings, accept the SQL ILIKE + JSONB-on-top_findings limitation in v1 (only the embedded top-10 findings per report are searchable) and defer per-finding indexing to a follow-up FEAT, or expand the catalog schema with a findings child table now?
plausible_answers: Accept v1 limitation; document it in the tool description and §1 Non-Goals. Defer per-finding indexing + PgVector to a follow-up FEAT. [Recommended — matches brainstorm §9 #5 lean]
Add a `security_findings` child table now (1:N from reports) with severity/title/resource columns. Costs another schema design pass but unlocks proper search.
Skip search_findings entirely in v1; rely only on find_security_report metadata. Cuts scope but loses one of the four read-side tools.
unknowns.user_answer: Accept v1 limitation: SQL ILIKE + JSONB on top_findings is the v1 query path. Documented in tool description and §1 Non-Goals. Per-finding indexing + PgVector deferred to a follow-up FEAT.

recommended_next_command.rationale: Localization is high-confidence, the architectural approach in the brainstorm is sound after verification, and the corrections needed are tactical (paths, method names, kwargs) rather than architectural. /sdd-spec can produce ordered tasks immediately once U1-U4 are answered in its §3 clarifying round.

meta.notes: All 23 findings cited. Research confirmed every brainstorm architectural pillar but corrected ~10 codebase contract errors. The brainstorm pre-dates today's FEAT-160 merge and the April 2026 fileinterface-migration; both are now baked into the constraints. Overall confidence is high because corrections are concrete (specific paths and method names from direct reads), not speculative.
